title: Lead, Sustainability
description:
• Translate global sustainability developments and regional trends into business-relevant insights that support the Group’s long-term vision and well-being mandate.
  • Advise on emerging sustainability risks and opportunities and ensure integration into group-level planning and positioning.
  • Maintain a forward-looking, impact-focused approach that evolves with stakeholder expectations, regulatory shifts, and innovation.
  • Maintain sustainability governance frameworks, internal policies, and reporting structures in alignment with global standards (e.g.GRI, ISSB, SBTi, IFC PS, GIIN, etc.) and local regulatory requirements.
  • Support the sustainability reporting process, working closely with relevant departments to collect, analyse, and articulate sustainability performance and disclosures.
  • Support the group sustainability council and conduct meetings once every 2 months to promote sustainability awareness and monitoring of projects’ performance.
  • Perform periodic internal reviews and improvement cycles to ensure sustainability metrics are actionable, transparent, and decision-enabling.
  • Review and approve investment team’s ESG downsides and impact upsides across due diligence and portfolio review.
  • Guide sustainability integration into new business ventures and innovation projects, particularly those related to green or regenerative models.
  • Align sustainability priorities with the Group’s strategic direction, without duplication of brand or marketing functions.
  • Cultivate high-impact partnerships with regulators, industrial experts, and regional and global institutions.
  • Represent the Group in sustainability forums and regulatory dialogues to build visibility and credibility.
  • Engage with philanthropic, academic, and civic partners to drive joint initiatives and knowledge exchange.
  • Build sustainability literacy across the Group through internal engagement, capacity-building programs such as CSR initiatives, and thought leadership.
  • Act as an internal advisor to functions implementing sustainability-related workstreams such as Restore Nature and Wellbeing and EESG Alliance (WEGA).
  • Promote a mindset of conscious leadership and collective responsibility through clear sustainability narratives and values alignment.
  • Contribute to shaping the culture around long-termism, stewardship, and ethical growth

Job Requirement:

  • Minimum 10-15 years of experience in sustainability, ESG, or impact strategy roles, ideally with experience across both business and investment environments in Asia.
  • Familiarity with global sustainability frameworks (e.g. GRI, ISSB, SBTi, IFC PS, GIIN, etc.) and experience implementing governance or reporting structures.
  • Strong cross-functional collaboration experience, particularly with investments, strategy, or transformation teams.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead sustainability initiatives independently while aligning with broader business or brand direction.
  • Prior exposure to sectors such as logistics, maritime, energy transition, or purpose-led investment is beneficial.

Soft Skills:

  • Proficient in English; Mandarin is advantageous for regional engagement.
  • Strong communication, facilitation, and stakeholder engagement skills across internal and external audiences.
  • Capable of articulating sustainability in strategic, operational, and brand-aligned language.
  • Proactive, adaptable, and able to operate effectively in a matrixed and evolving organizational environment.

Education:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Sustainability, Environmental Management, Business, or related disciplines.
  • Certifications in ESG, sustainability reporting, or climate governance are a plus.
